audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for locusts, Pharaoh's servants' protest, partial release bargaining, darkness, and final rupture.

verses.restored: YHWH said to Moses, Come to Pharaoh, for I have made his heart heavy and heart of his servants, so that I may set these signs of mine in his midst,
and so that you may recount in ears of your child and your child's child how I dealt with Egypt and my signs that I set among them, and you shall know that I am YHWH.
Moses and Aaron came to Pharaoh and said to him, Thus says YHWH, Divine of Hebrews: Until when will you refuse to humble yourself before me? Send my people away, so they may serve/worship me.
For if you refuse to send my people, look, tomorrow I bring locusts into your border.
They will cover eye of land so no one can see the land, and they will eat remnant left to you from hail, and eat every tree sprouting for you from the field.

They will fill your houses, houses of all your servants, and houses of all Egypt, which your fathers and fathers of your fathers did not see from day they were on ground until this day. He turned and went out from Pharaoh.
Pharaoh's servants said to him, Until when will this one be a snare to us? Send the men, so they may serve/worship YHWH their Divine. Do you not yet know that Egypt is ruined?
Moses and Aaron were brought back to Pharaoh, and he said to them, Go, serve/worship YHWH your Divine. Who and who are going?
Moses said, With our young and our old we will go; with our sons and daughters, with our flock and herd we will go, because we have festival to YHWH.
He said to them, May YHWH be with you if I send you and your little ones. See, harm is before your faces.
Not so. Go now, the men, and serve/worship YHWH, for that is what you seek. And they were driven out from Pharaoh's face.
YHWH said to Moses, Stretch your hand over land of Egypt for locusts, and they shall go up over land of Egypt and eat every plant of land, all that hail left.
Moses stretched his staff over land of Egypt, and YHWH drove east wind through the land all that day and all night; morning came, and east wind carried the locusts.
The locusts went up over all land of Egypt and rested in all border of Egypt, very heavy. Before them there had not been such locust, and after them there will not be so.
They covered eye of all land, and land became dark; they ate every plant of land and all fruit of trees left by hail, and no green thing remained on tree or plant of field in all land of Egypt.
Pharaoh hurried to call Moses and Aaron and said, I have sinned/misaligned against YHWH your Divine and against you.
Now please lift my sin/misalignment just this once, and plead to YHWH your Divine, that he remove from me only this death.
He went out from Pharaoh and pleaded to YHWH.
YHWH turned a very strong west wind, and it lifted the locusts and drove them into Sea of Reeds; not one locust remained in all border of Egypt.
But YHWH strengthened/hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not send the children of Israel.
YHWH said to Moses, Stretch your hand toward heavens, and there will be darkness over land of Egypt, and darkness may be felt.
Moses stretched his hand toward heavens, and thick darkness was in all land of Egypt three days.
They did not see each man his brother, and no one rose from under himself three days; but for all children of Israel there was light in their dwellings.
Pharaoh called to Moses and said, Go, serve/worship YHWH; only your flock and herd shall stay. Even your little ones may go with you.
Moses said, You yourself must also give into our hand sacrifices and burnt offerings, and we will make them for YHWH our Divine.

And also our livestock shall go with us; not a hoof shall remain, for from it we must take to serve/worship YHWH our Divine, and we do not know with what we must serve/worship YHWH until we come there.
YHWH strengthened/hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he was not willing to send them.
Pharaoh said to him, Go from me. Guard yourself; do not see my face again, for in day you see my face you shall die.
Moses said, As you have spoken: I will not see your face again.

translation_notes: The signs become intergenerational memory: tell your child and child's child.
Pharaoh's servants now protest Pharaoh's refusal; this again blocks ethnic flattening.
Pharaoh repeatedly bargains for partial liberation: men only, then people without livestock. Moses insists total release for worship-service.
safety_note: Locust and darkness signs should be read within the liberation confrontation. Do not celebrate ecological devastation; note the text's own concern that Egypt is ruined by Pharaoh's refusal.
